title: schiedeella transversalis
intro: Schiedeella transversalis, also known as the "Hawaiian Baby Woodrose," is a plant species native to Hawaii and India, belonging to the Asclepiadaceae family. This plant contains ergine, a psychoactive compound, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Schiedeella transversalis include its ability to induce vivid dreams, reduce anxiety, and promote relaxation, making it a popular herb for spiritual and meditative practices. Herbal preparations of Schiedeella transversalis typically involve drying and processing the plant's roots and leaves, which can be consumed as a tea or used in smudging ceremonies.
